title: The real reason so many devs are getting laid off in 2025
post:
Every tech company is laying off developers. Here's what's actually happening.

It's not a recession. It's not AI taking jobs. It's a structural shift in how software is built.

For the first time in 30 years, software development productivity is increasing faster than demand. One developer with AI can now do what used to take five. And companies are realizing they over-hired during the 2021 boom.

The layoffs aren't random. They're targeting mid-level developers who replaced senior judgment with AI output. The seniors who use AI well are still getting paid premium.

If you're a developer today and you haven't figured out how to use AI as a multiplier, you're competing in a market where demand is shrinking and supply is increasing.

This isn't fear-mongering. It's a structural shift that favors developers who adapt.

How are you adapting? Thoughts?
